# Dotfiles Git Hygiene

Use this skill when the task is repo-local branch cleanup, worktree cleanup, or managed Codex worktree residue.

## Default workflow

1. Read `dotfiles://workflows/repo-hygiene` before mutating anything.
2. Run `dotfiles_repo_git_hygiene` in dry-run mode first, usually with `cleanup_local_merged=true` and `cleanup_worktrees=true`.
3. Inspect blocked or dirty candidates before turning on `execute=true`.
4. Only enable remote branch deletion after verifying the merged remote list is actually safe.
5. Use `prune_managed_state=true` when `.codex/worktrees` or Codex-managed state is part of the repo debt.
6. Re-run the repo baseline when cleanup overlaps with merge or integration work.

## Guardrails

- Prefer `branch_prefixes` when you only want to target one branch family.
- Do not delete dirty or unmerged worktrees through forceful shell cleanup.
- Treat managed worktree pruning as a second phase after the plain git scan is understood.
